programme.title: Southern and Hungry: Smokey Mountain Majesty
programme.desc: Damaris Philips and Rutledge Wood continue their tour in Knoxville, Tennessee, a city that insists on using only the freshest regional ingredients for its spectacular Southern cuisine.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Weekend Staycation
programme.desc: A weekend staycation brunch at Chef Bobby Flay's home! Bobby's kitchen is the destination for a cozy menu of crunchy maple topped oatmeal, avocado salmon benedict, cinnamon sugar mini pancake muffins.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: The Big Easy
programme.desc: Inspired by what he calls one of the greatest food cities in America, Bobby creates a brunch menu that would make New Orleans proud.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Chocoholic Brunch
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay is covering the kitchen in chocolate for a luscious menu of sweet and savory treats like dark chocolate waffles with white chocolate custard sauce and espresso-chocolate whipped cream.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Brunch on the Run
programme.desc: A busy weekend schedule doesn't prevent Chef Bobby Flay from enjoying the brunch experience, even on the go. Take anywhere dishes include cinnamon sugar French toast sticks with maple cream, and a pressed egg sandwich.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Smoked Whole Hog
programme.desc: Michael Symon smokes a whole hog for a juicy pulled pork sandwich in Asheville, North Carolina.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Mountains Of Meat
programme.desc: Michael Symon meets a chef bringing the Indian street food flavors of his childhood to Asheville, N.C., with his own take on a sloppy joe that he calls a Sloppy Jai, with lamb hash and 30 Indian spices.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Smokehouse Reinventions
programme.desc: In Islip, N.Y., Michael Symon digs into a decadent, 17-layer smokehouse reinvention of lasagna with pork, brisket, bacon and mac and cheese. Then in Buffalo, a brewpub serves up the city's most famous export, buffalo chicken.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Meat Lover's Madness
programme.desc: Michael Symon heads to his hometown of Cleveland to visit a brewery that revolutionizes classic pub fare with decadent confit chicken wings in chipotle-maple glaze.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Close Encounters Of The Food Kind

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Planet of the Funnel Cakes

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Grill Bill
programme.desc: Noah Cappe travels to the Great Lakes to visit the Barry County Fair.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Fry Harder
programme.desc: Noah Cappe soaks up the California sunshine while enjoying some wacky West Coast treats at the San Diego County Fair in Del Mar, California.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: The Postman Always Brings Rice
programme.desc: Host Noah Cappe heads to Hawaii for the Maui Fair where rice is always on the menu. He enjoys traditional Hawaiian treats like the Laulau Feast and Loco Moco and cross-cultural favs like Furikake Salmon.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Pepper Popper Party
programme.desc: Noah Cappe challenges carnival concessionaires across America to feature everyone's favorite spicy bar snack in their offerings. From hot dogs and burgers to pizza and onion rings, even a dessert, these pepper popper creations are on fire.

programme.desc: Pip and Freddy deliver a puppy who seems like a perfect angel, but turns out to be a mischievous rascal. Pip and Freddy pretend to be knights while delivering a Komodo dragon.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Rock-A-Bye Birdie/ The Fly-Along
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy must impress Captain Beakman when she joins them for a ride-a-long delivery. When Mimi the mockingbird sings K.C.'s lullaby she puts everyone at T.O.T.S. to sleep.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: Blame It on the Rain/ Busted Boomer
programme.desc: Alma accidentally leaves her stuffed cow Moo Moo outside on a rainy day. Doc helps cure a deflated soccer ball with what is diagnosed as 'Deflate-ulosis'.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The Total Yodel / Bob's Birthday Wish
programme.desc: After Bob has trouble learning to yodel, the Pugs decide to go on a mission to the Swiss Alps to get yodeling tips. It’s Bob’s birthday and his sister, Bonnie, is acting sneaky.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Tour Bus/ Game Show
programme.desc: When Alvin talks Dave into buying a used tour bus, Dave has no idea of the problems ahead. Alvin signs the Chipmunks up for a game show that forces losing contestants to perform dangerous stunts.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Dog Days/ Dragon Dad
programme.desc: The Chipmunks take in a stray dog, but have to keep the puppy hidden from Dave, who won't allow pets. Theodore orders a dragon egg in the hopes of it hatching into a real dragon.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Suck Toad/ Secret Admirer
programme.desc: Brittany is preparing a class speech on 'inner beauty' when an endangered species makes its home on her face. Alvin believes that Kevin has a secret crush on one of the Chipettes.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Curiosity/ House Pest
programme.desc: Alvin gets a taste of his own medicine when he pulls one too many practical jokes on Simon.When Miss Croner finds termites have infested her home, she moves in with the chipmunks and drives everyone crazy.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: iHear/ Switch Witch
programme.desc: Alvin gets a device that lets him listen in on everyone's conversations. When the kids' Halloween candy disappears, they're sure the culprit is 'Switch Witch.'

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Mojo Missing/ Who's the Animal
programme.desc: Alvin is convinced his mojo was in a cap he gave to a fan, and must find a way to get his hat back before the Chipmunks' big concert. During a costume visit to the zoo, Alvin finds himself mistaken for the real Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: A Wish Too Far/ Tiny Timmy
programme.desc: Timmy wishes for an abundance of material possessions in an attempt to become popular.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Father Time/ Apartnership
programme.desc: Timmy time-travels to prevent his father from winning a trophy; Cosmo and Wanda split up.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Boys in the Band/ Hex Games
programme.desc: Vicky has kidnapped teen singing sensation Chip Skylark, and a stadium full of screaming girls is going to tear the town apart if he doesn't perform for them. Timmy challenges Vicky as Queen of the Skatepark.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Action Packed/ Smarty Pants
programme.desc: Timmy wishes his life could be like an action movie, and that he could know everything.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Friend or Foe
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs and Plankton have been business rivals and bitter enemies for as long as anyone can remember, but Mr. Krabs explains that this wasn't always the case. Flashbacks reveal how their friendship went sour.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Hello Bikini Bottom!
programme.desc: A concert promoter wants to send SpongeBob and Squidward on the road, until greedy Mr. Krabs decides to manage them and their 'world tour,' himself.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: New Digs/ Krabs a la Mode
programme.desc: SpongeBob moves into the Krusty Krab and turns it into an ice skating rink.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Patrick! The Game/ The Sewers of Bikini Bottom
programme.desc: Patrick invents a new boardgame. Later, SpongeBob and Squidward flush Mr. Krabs' safe and must go into the sewers to find it.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Whale of a Birthday/ Karate Island
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs wants to give his daughter the best birthday ever. SpongeBob is assigned the task of finding out what she would like; SpongeBob is invited to a karate competition on the mysterious Karate Island.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Sharks vs. Pods/ CopyBob DittoPants
programme.desc: SpongeBob gets in over his head with the Sharks. Plankton creates an evil clone of SpongeBob.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Goo Goo Gas/ Le Big Switch
programme.desc: Plankton creates a gas that turns everyone at the Krusty Krab into babies. After being enrolled in a cooks exchange program, SpongeBob switches places with a snooty French chef from a high-end restaurant.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Fish Bowl/ Married to Money
programme.desc: Sandy attempts to study Patrick and SpongeBob. Mr. Krab announces he's getting married.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Picture Day/ Pat No Pay/ Blackjack
programme.desc: SpongeBob must attend Picture Day for the Boating School Year Book; Patrick steals.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Sold!/ Lame and Fortune
programme.desc: When SpongeBob and Patrick think their homes have been sold, they move away. When fortune cookies start predicting the future, Plankton realizes he can twist them to his own evil schemes.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Inmates of Summer/ To Save a Squirrel
programme.desc: Instead of getting on the ship headed for summer camp, SpongeBob and Patrick accidentally board a ship full of prisoners on their way to jail on Inferno Island.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Goodbye, Krabby Patty?
programme.desc: Squidward must make Patrick and SpongeBob friends again after they learn that sponges and sea stars aren't ususally friends.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: 20,000 Patties Under the Sea/ The Battle of Bikini Bottom
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Mr. Krabs turn an old submarine into a mobile Krusty Krab. When Patrick and SpongeBob discover they have clashing views on cleanliness, their disagreement blows up into an epic battle.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Home Sweet Rubble
programme.desc: SpongeBob asks for help from the community to remodel his home.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Two Faces of Squidward/ Spongehenge
programme.desc: SpongeBob slams the door in Squidward's face; Squidward gets surgery and becomes handsome.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Graveyard Shift/ Krusty Love
programme.desc: The Krusty Krab is now open 24 hours a day; Mr. Krabs is smitten with Ms. Puff.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: House Fancy/ Krabby Road
programme.desc: SpongeBob helps Squidward redecorate his house. / Plankton forms a rock band with SpongeBob.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Yours, Mine and Mine/ Kracked Krabs
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick buy a toy and have to learn to share it.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Spongicus/ Suction Cup Symphony
programme.desc: Plankton builds a Roman coliseum, and puts on a free gladiator show featuring togas, chariots, and ferocious lionfish. / Squidward is determined to win the Bikini Bottom Symphony's composition contest, but SpongeBob and Patrick keep distracting him.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: SpongeBob's Last Stand
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick protest the construction of a highway that would destroy Jellyfish Fields.

programme.title: The Loud House: Predict Ability / Driving Ambition
programme.desc: Lincoln fears he's predictable, so vows to change up his routine. Then, Lori gets the yips when she learns the golf coach from her dream college is coming to see her play.

programme.title: The Loud House: Intern for the Worse/ The Old and the Restless
programme.desc: Lincoln and Clyde both get an internship at Flip's and compete to win the manager title. When Lincoln sees how tame Pop Pop has gotten in his older age, he takes him out for a day to remember.

programme.title: The Loud House: Net Gains/ Pipe Dreams
programme.desc: Lynn desperately wants to win a basketball championship but gets drafted onto a terrible team. Mom and Dad are sick of waiting in the long bathroom line so they decide to build a secret bathroom.

programme.title: The Loud House: Suite and Sour/ Back in Black
programme.desc: After Mom and Dad win a trip to a resort, they decide to take the kids. When Lucy gets a crush on a 'normal' boy, her sisters give her a makeover.

programme.title: The Loud House: Fandom Pains/ Rita Her Rights
programme.desc: Lucy is annoyed that Lori and Leni have started watching her vampire show because of a cute new character. After getting community service for racking up tickets, Mom finds it's more relaxing than being at home.

programme.title: The Loud House: Fed Up/ Shell Shock
programme.desc: The kids make a plan to sabotage dinner so they can have pizza. Lincoln and Ronnie Anne have to take care of an egg baby, but Lincoln doesn't trust her with the fragile egg.

programme.title: The Loud House: Legends/ Mall of Duty
programme.desc: Dad and Lincoln get on 'Legends of the Hidden Temple' and are excited for a day of bonding. Lincoln wants to go to the mall and has to bring his sisters along.

programme.title: The Loud House: Lock 'N Loud/ The Whole Picture
programme.desc: Dad gets upset with the kids for never locking the front door; Lincoln accidentally erases his childhood photos.

programme.title: The Loud House: The Loudest Mission: Relative Chaos
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne and Bobby visit their crazy, chaotic extended family. Bobby fits in great but Ronnie Anne struggles with the smothering nature of their relatives. Little do they know, their mother has a secret motive.

programme.title: The Loud House: Future Tense/ Lynner Takes All
programme.desc: When Mom and Dad meet an impressive family, they worry their kids aren't doing enough to ensure successful futures. Tired of Lynn's poor sportsmanship during board games, the siblings decide to team up to take her down.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Finding Emo/ Anchor's Away
programme.desc: Timmy likes a new girl at school. He wishes he was 'emo' and stops caring about everything. Foop and Poof have to take care of an egg for a school project. Foop breaks it and swaps it with a dangerous one.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Blondas Have More Fun/ Five Days of Flarg
programme.desc: Wanda and her sister Blonda switch bodies; Mark the Alien is missing Flarg on his planet.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Timmy's Secret Wish
programme.desc: Timmy makes his millionth wish so all of Fairyworld has gathered to celebrate. But after reviewing all the wishes Timmy has made, the Fairy Council finds that he should be put on trial for being the worst godkid ever.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Operation Fun/ Smart Attack
programme.desc: Remy Buxaplenty has come home from military school for spring break. All he can talk about is how great his new school is, making Timmy and friends jealous; Dad keeps trying to help Timmy with his homework, but he's not the brightest dad.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Spaced Out/ Transparents
programme.desc: Timmy wishes for an authentic alien; Cosmo and Wanda must pose as real parents.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Wishology: The Beginning
programme.desc: Centuries ago, fairy ancestors hid a mystical wand on Earth that has the power to defeat The Darkness, but only the Chosen One depicted in the Fairy World prophecy can unlock its mysteries. Timmy gets chosen for the task, as he resembles the prophecy.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: A New Squid in Town/ Wish Fixers
programme.desc: Mark Chang has to be protected from a forced wedding; Timmy's on a bad wishmaking streak.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Back to the Norm/ Teeth for Two
programme.desc: Crocker and a genie share a mortal enemy; Jorgen tries to pull Timmy's tooth out.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Society of the Blind Eye
programme.desc: The kids, along with Old Man McGucket's help, discover there is a secret society on Gravity Falls.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Blendin's Game
programme.desc: Blendin Blandin returns to face off with Dipper and Mabel in a futuristic battle that could trap them forever in time jail.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Love God
programme.desc: Mabel takes matchmaking too far when she steals a love potion from a real Love God.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Not What He Seems
programme.desc: Government agents take Stan into custody, making Dipper and Mabel question how much they really know about their Grunkle Stan.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Northwest Mansion Mystery
programme.desc: Pacifica asks Dipper to bust a poltergeist haunting the Northwest Mansion, before he wreaks havoc on her fancy party.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Dungeons, Dungeons and More Dungeons
programme.desc: Dipper finds an unlikely friend to join him with his newest obsession, a board game called 'Dungeons, Dungeons & More Dungeons.'

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Stanchurian Candidate
programme.desc: When Grunkle Stan decides to run for mayor, Dipper and Mabel have their work cut out for them trying to turn their gaffe-prone uncle into the perfect candidate.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Roadside Attraction
programme.desc: Grunkle Stan takes the kids with him on a road-trip to sabotage all the other tourist traps in Oregon.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Dipper and Mabel vs. the Future
programme.desc: Mabel decides to plan for her and Dipper's 13th birthday party. Meanwhile, Dipper ventures below the town to find its most shocking secret.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Last Mabelcorn
programme.desc: A new threat leads Mabel to venture into the enchanted realm of the unicorns. Meanwhile, Dipper learns an unexpected twist about the enigmatic Bill Cipher.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Weirdmageddon
programme.desc: Dipper and Mabel Pines are sent to spend the summer with their Great Uncle Sam.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Between the Pines
programme.desc: Dipper and Mabel Pines are sent to spend the summer with their Great Uncle Sam.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Inconveniencing
programme.desc: When Dipper tries to impress Wendy by acting older than he really is, he's invited to join a group of teenagers on a trek that leads to an old abandoned convenience store that could be haunted.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Time Traveler's Pig
programme.desc: When Dipper wishes he could go back in time and undo a mistake he made, he discovers there is a time machine that can help him do just that. Meanwhile, it's love at first sight for Mabel when she wins a pet pig at the fair.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Double Dipper
programme.desc: When Stan throws a big weekend party to promote the Mystery Shack, Dipper uses an off-kilter copy machine to make clones of himself so he can impress Wendy. Meanwhile, Mabel stands up for her friends and fights for the Party Queen title in their honour.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boss Mabel
programme.desc: When Mabel questions the way Sam runs the Shack, he challenges her to a bet: whoever can make the most money in three days wins the chance to run the Shack however they wish for the rest of the summer.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Fight Fighters
programme.desc: After being challenged to a video game battle by Wendy's boyfriend Robbie, Dipper is shocked when his favourite playable fighter, Rumble McSkirmish, comes to life to defend him. Dipper soon loses control over Rumble and has to find a way to stop him.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Land Before Swine
programme.desc: When a prehistoric beast snatches Mabel’s beloved pig Waddles, the Pines family must journey to a land trapped in time to rescue him.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Deep End
programme.desc: When Mabel befriends a cute Merman trapped in the deep end of the public pool, she's determined to return him to his family in the ocean, even though that means her whirlwind romance will come to an end.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Into the Bunker
programme.desc: Dipper's secret journal pages lead him and the gang to the author's hidden bunker where they find themselves face-to-face with a mysterious figure from Gravity Falls' lore.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Love God
programme.desc: Mabel takes matchmaking too far when she steals a love potion from a real Love God.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Blendin's Game
programme.desc: Blendin Blandin returns to face off with Dipper and Mabel in a futuristic battle that could trap them forever in time jail.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Not What He Seems
programme.desc: Government agents take Stan into custody, making Dipper and Mabel question how much they really know about their Grunkle Stan.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Stanchurian Candidate
programme.desc: When Grunkle Stan decides to run for mayor, Dipper and Mabel have their work cut out for them trying to turn their gaffe-prone uncle into the perfect candidate.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Roadside Attraction
programme.desc: Grunkle Stan takes the kids with him on a road-trip to sabotage all the other tourist traps in Oregon.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Hand That Rocks the Mabel
programme.desc: Dipper and Mabel discover their seemingly cherubic new neighbour, Lil' Gideon, happens to be the town's adored psychic. But when Dipper convinces Mabel to go on a date with Gideon to question his authenticity, she soon learns that his cuteness can fade.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Little Dipper
programme.desc: Dipper feels self-conscious that Mabel is taller than him and is becoming the alpha twin, so he seeks out a magic way to grow himself the same height. Lil' Gideon takes advantage of Dipper's and Mabel's height discrepancies.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Day of the Living Gelatin!/ Elementary, My Dear Stacy
programme.desc: Pool filled with bouncy gelatin and it comes to life; later family takes trip to England.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: We Call It Maze/ Ladies and Gentlemen, Meet Max Modem!
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb create a life sized maze filled with fun obstacles. But things don't go as planned when the Little Sparks try out the maze. Lawrence asks the boys to help him turn into a pop star so he can perform with Linda at a Lindana concert.

programme.title: Amphibia: Handy Anne/ Fort in the Road
programme.desc: Worried that something bad could happen while they are in Newtopia, Anne decides to disaster-proof the farm. Later, Sprig longs for exotic road trip adventures, but Hop Pop's strict road rules threaten to spoil all of his fun plans.

programme.title: Amphibia: Family Fishing Trip/ Bizarre Bazaar
programme.desc: Sprig wants to spend time with Hop Pop on their family fishing trip, but Hop Pop's friend Sylvia gets in the way. Anne's music box goes missing at the mysterious and exclusive Bizarre Bazaar.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Matchmaker/ School Spirit
programme.desc: In an effort to get a better grade on her math test, Star offers to help her teacher Miss Skullnick with her love life but accidentally turns her into a troll. Star sets booby traps to protect the football team.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Monster Arm/ The Other Exchange Student
programme.desc: When Marco breaks his arm before a big karate tournament, Star tries to fix it with magic but accidentally turns it into a giant tentacle. The Diazes are visited by their charming former exchange student Gustav, and Star quickly becomes jealous.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Mewberty/ Pixtopia
programme.desc: Marco and his friends run up Star’s Magic Mirror bill with prank phone calls, so they all travel to a dimension of pixies to pay it off. Star starts sprouting hearts all over her body, the telltale sign of Mewberty.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Lobster Claws/ Sleep Spells
programme.desc: Ludo fires one of his minions, Lobster Claws, so Marco and Star help him adjust to life on Earth. Marco uses psychology to help Star when she starts using magic in her sleep.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Blood Moon Ball/ Fortune Cookies
programme.desc: Star accepts her ex-boyfriend Tom's invitation to Demon Prom, but Marco is suspicious of Tom's intentions and crashes the prom to prevent Star from getting her heart broken again.

programme.title: Star vs. the Forces of Evil: Mewnipendance Day/ The Banagic Incident
programme.desc: Star leads a battle re-enactment as part of a Mewni Independence Day celebration with her friends on Earth. Then, Star sees a commercial for a magical Earth product and sets off on her own to buy it.

programme.title: The Owl House: I Was a Teenage Abomination
programme.desc: Luz sneaks into the local magic school to help a friend and makes a new enemy in the process.

programme.title: The Owl House: Covention
programme.desc: Luz learns about witch covens, but her lesson goes awry when she finds herself thrust into a witch's duel!

programme.title: The Owl House: Hooty's Moving Hassle
programme.desc: When Luz, Willow and Gus accidentally animate the Owl House, the house runs amok around Bonesborough.

programme.title: The Owl House: Once Upon a Swap
programme.desc: A simple disagreement leads to a complex situation when Eda, King, and Luz triple-down on a wager.

programme.title: The Owl House: Understanding Willow

programme.title: Movie: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay, Part 2
programme.desc: The story comes to a close as Katniss and the unit from District 13 prepare to take down President Snow once and for all. With the nation in a full-scale war, Katniss will be tested as never before as she fights for the very future of Panem.

programme.title: Movie: Matilda
programme.desc: An intelligent young girl is sent away to a school with a mean principal and a very nice teacher. While there, Matilda develops her telekinetic powers and uses them to trick the mean adults in her life.

programme.title: Movie: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales
programme.desc: Captain Jack Sparrow is thrust into a new adventure when a crew of ghost sailors under the command of his old nemesis, Captain Salazar, takes over the ocean. Jack must find the Trident of Poseidon in order to save his own life and defeat Salazar.

programme.title: Movie: Beauty and the Beast
programme.desc: A young woman named Belle longs to leave her tiny village. When she discovers her father is missing, she finds him as a prisoner of a Beast. She takes his place and lives at the castle, drawing closer to Beast but unaware of the approaching danger.

programme.title: Movie: The Wizard of Oz
programme.desc: A tornado carries a young girl to a magical land where she encounters witches and magnificent wonders.

programme.title: Bones: The Carrot in the Kudzu
programme.desc: When a children's TV show actor is found dead, the team goes behind the scenes to uncover who murdered everyone's favourite character, 'Carrot Bill.'

programme.title: Bones: The Turn in the Urn
programme.desc: When a wealthy artifact collector walks into his own funeral, the Jeffersonian team must find out to whom the cremated remains at the funeral belong. The investigation gets complicated when the team finds there are three sets of remains in the urn.

programme.title: Bones: The High in the Low
programme.desc: The Jeffersonian team investigates the death of an art school dropout with Lupus, whose remains were found inside a log in a forest. The team learns about the victim's use of cannabis to treat her pain, and uncover details that contributed to her death.

programme.title: Charmed: Black As Cole
programme.desc: Phoebe comes face-to-face with Cole's demonic past when a widow tracks him down seeking vengeance for her husband's murder. Elsewhere, Paige encourages Piper and Leo to test their readiness to become parents.

programme.title: Charmed: Muse to My Ears
programme.desc: As Phoebe, Paige and Piper attempt to stop warlocks from enslaving the world's muses for evil purposes, Cole fears that without his demonic powers he can no longer protect Phoebe.

programme.title: Charmed: A Paige From the Past
programme.desc: Leo takes Paige, who is hunted by her parent's tragic death, back in time to relive the events leading up to the car accident. Meanwhile, ghosts from the 1950s take possession of Phoebe and Cole's bodies.

programme.title: Charmed: Trial by Magic
programme.desc: When Phoebe has a premonition that the defendant in a murder trial is innocent, Piper, Paige and Leo frantically try to discover the real killer in time to free the wrongly accused man.

programme.title: Supernatural: Breakdown
programme.desc: Donna calls Sam and Dean for help when her niece Wendy goes missing. The three discover that she was kidnapped by a man selling body parts to monsters in a grotesque online auction and race to save her before it's too late.

programme.title: Supernatural: Various and Sundry Villains
programme.desc: Dean falls victim to sister witches Jamie and Jennie Plum who manage to steal a powerful book of spells from the Winchester brothers. Sam and Dean go after the book with help from a powerful and surprising ally in Rowena, back from the dead.

programme.title: Supernatural: Devil's Bargain
programme.desc: Sam, Dean and Castiel search for Lucifer who's made an unlikely deal with a local faith healer, Sister Jo. Asmodeus inches closer to finding Jack.

programme.title: Supernatural: Good Intentions
programme.desc: Jack and Mary escape Michael and find an ally in 'apocalyptic world' Bobby Singer. Sam, Dean and Castiel search for a way to open a breach not realizing that one of their own may be working against them, costing them precious time.

programme.title: Movie: Need for Speed
programme.desc: Two years after being framed and imprisoned for manslaughter, a blue-collar mechanic joins a cross-country car race in the hopes of exacting revenge. With a bounty placed on his car by the man who framed him, he must dodge the authorities at every turn.

programme.desc: The honeymoon is over for Gina when Martin stays out one night, playing pool and getting hustled until he loses even the clothes off his back.

programme.desc: A group of friends comes together for a wedding in Las Vegas. Various mishaps and misadventures lead the gang into some very sticky situations, and what was supposed to be a romantic weekend away begins to veer in another direction.

programme.desc: Two couples immediately end up together, respectively, after meeting in a bar. They soon find their new relationships put to the test when they must take things out of the bedroom and into the real world.

programme.title: Movie: Booty Call
programme.desc: Plans for romance go awry for two young men when they go searching for prophylactics.

programme.title: Living Single: Rags to Riches

programme.desc: Khadijah and Synclaire disagree on how to spend a mature savings bond.

programme.title: Living Single: The Handyman Can
programme.desc: A bookshelf that Overton built collapses, killing a cat in Synclaire's charge.

programme.title: Living Single: He Works Hard for the Money
programme.desc: Kyle is uncomfortable with the gifts and attention he's receiving from a famous client.

programme.title: Martin: Kill Him with Kindness
programme.desc: Martin thinks Gina wants to kill him for life insurance money.

programme.title: Martin: Blow, Baby, Blow
programme.desc: When music producer Biggie Smalls comes to town, everybody wants to be a star.

programme.title: Martin: Ring a Ding, Ding, Ding Gone
programme.desc: Martin takes off his wedding ring when his pals complain that it scares off women, but he's the frightened one when he has to explain its loss to Gina.

programme.title: Martin: Love T.K.O.
programme.desc: Martin and Gina's romantic weekend gets interrupted by everyone else's love woes.

programme.desc: When Will witnesses a murder, he and his family are put in the witness protection program.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Will's Misery

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Father Knows Best
programme.desc: Ashley realizes that there is more to life than the controlled environment she lives in.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Sooooooooul Train
programme.desc: Philip and Vivian have been named one of the 25 most memorable couples of Soul Train.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Will's ego takes a beating when Lisa has to beat up a guy for him.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Will's Up the Dirt Road
programme.desc: Will gets sued while trying to impress Lisa by taking pictures of celebrity homes.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Will Steps Out
programme.desc: Will decides to fight fire-with-fire when he suspects that Lisa is dating another guy.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Same Game, Next Season
programme.desc: Lisa's father is so impressed with Will that he intrudes on their budding relationship.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: Silence Is Golden
programme.desc: Michael accompanies Jay to a yoga and meditation weekend but he can't keep silent.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: Bahamas
programme.desc: Mike's relaxing vacation is disrupted when his obnoxious golf buddy Jimmy shows up.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: Bahamas 2
programme.desc: Michael and Jay find themselves stranded on an island with the tide rapidly rising.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: The Remodel
programme.desc: When Jay wants the garage remodelled, Michael and Jr. try to do the job themselves.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: Michael Joins a Gym
programme.desc: When Michael is unable to fit into his old jeans, he goes on an extreme fitness regimen.

programme.title: My Wife and Kids: Celibacy
programme.desc: Jay tells Michael that their love life will be better if they are celibate for six months.

programme.title: The Wayans Brothers: I Do?
programme.desc: Marlon proposes to his girlfriend when her threatening brother suggests this would be a good idea.

programme.title: The Wayans Brothers: Dee's Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Dee wants a baby and she wants Shawn as the dad.

programme.title: Movie: Pride, Prejudice and Mistletoe
programme.desc: A career-driven woman finds her life turned upside down when she is forced to return to her hometown to care for her sick mother.

programme.title: Movie: Write Before Christmas
programme.desc: A woman writes special Christmas cards to those who inspired her.

programme.title: Movie: A Timeless Christmas
programme.desc: A man travels from 1903 to 2020, where he meets a woman giving tours of his home and experiences a 21st Century Christmas.

programme.title: Movie: Never Kiss a Man in a Christmas Sweater
programme.desc: Single mom Maggie is facing Christmas alone until Lucas crashes into her life and becomes an unexpected houseguest. Together they overcome Christmas while finding comfort in their growing bond.

programme.title: Movie: Holiday Date
programme.desc: A man poses as a former boyfriend for a woman when she returns home for the holidays. As her family celebrates Christmas, he requests they also celebrate Hanukah and a merry ruse is born.

programme.title: Movie: Merry and Bright
programme.desc: Two people work together to make their candy-cane factory profitable, but in the end, it's their love that turns a profit.

programme.title: Movie: Christmas Scavenger Hunt
programme.desc: A woman heads home for the holidays, things get complicated and she is forced to work together with her ex for the towns annual scavenger hunt.

programme.title: Movie: Double Holiday
programme.desc: A woman is tasked with throwing her companies holiday party, with her nemesis. The two must work together to mingle work and traditions to make the party a success.

programme.title: Movie: Christmas by Starlight
programme.desc: When her family's beloved eatery, The Starlight Café, is slated for demolition, Annie vows to put a stop to it before Christmas.

programme.title: Movie: Christmas Next Door
programme.desc: The author of bachelor lifestyle books, is left in charge of his young niece and nephew for the holidays.

programme.title: Movie: Sleigh Bells Ring
programme.desc: A recently divorced woman struggles to get into the holiday spirit while heading up her hometown parade. With the help of a Christmas sleigh and a handsome woodworker, she is destined to turn her luck around.

programme.title: Movie: Christmas in Evergreen: Letters to Santa
programme.desc: A woman returns to her home town for Christmas and discovers that the town's historic general store has shut down. Using the skills she has as a retail designer, she is determined to bring the store back to life in time for the holidays.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The antics of pre-pubescent school children Kyle, Cartman, Kenny and Stan, as they stumble through the deranged misadventures of their hometown, South Park.

programme.title: South Park: Chef's Chocolate Salty Balls
programme.desc: South Park's first film festival attracts crowds of pretentious, tofu-eating movie lovers to the quiet mountain town. The influx of raw vegetable waste taxes the sewage system putting Mr. Hankey's life in jeopardy.

programme.title: South Park: Proper Condom Use
programme.desc: In a time-honoured tradition and right-of-passage, the boys are separated from the girls and schooled in the mysteries of sex by none other than Mr. Mackey.

programme.title: South Park: Chinpoko Mon
programme.desc: Stan, Kyle, Cartman and Kenny are caught up in the latest fad from Japan: Chinpoko Mon. Little do they know, they are being brainwashed by the Japanese to once again bomb Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: South Park: Gnomes
programme.desc: Cartman, Stan, Kyle and Kenny are assigned to write a report with Tweek, the very nervous and highly caffeinated boy who insists gnomes are stealing his underpants.

programme.title: South Park: The Ring
programme.desc: Kenny takes his new girlfriend to a Jonas Brothers' concert where they each get purity rings.

programme.title: South Park: The City Part of Town
programme.desc: The town of South Park is gentrifying and Kenny thinks it's time to get a job.

programme.title: South Park: The Poor Kid
programme.desc: Kenny ends up in the foster care system after his parents are arrested.

programme.title: South Park: Kenny Dies
programme.desc: When Kenny contracts a deadly disease, Cartman refuses to let his friend die. He speaks before Congress in favour of stem-cell research that could potentially save Kenny's life.

programme.title: South Park: A Ladder to Heaven
programme.desc: Kenny died with the winning ticket to a prize of endless candy in his pocket. The boys decide to build a ladder to Heaven where they expect to find him and get the ticket back. The Feds think Saddam may be hiding weapons in Heaven and plan to bomb it.

programme.title: South Park: Towelie
programme.desc: When the government steals their new Gamesphere, the boys will stop at nothing to get it back. There is also a new dysfunctional character in South Park who tries to recruit the boys to his cause.

programme.title: South Park: Conjoined Fetus Lady
programme.desc: Kyle visits the dreaded school nurse, who suffers from Conjoined Twin Myslexia Disease. His reaction prompts the entire town to rally behind the nurse, singling her out to 'honour' her. Meanwhile, Pip leads the school dodgeball team to the championships.

programme.title: South Park: Sexual Harassment Panda
programme.desc: After Sexual Harassment Panda 'educates' the children, Cartman sues Stan for sexual harassment, and a flurry of other lawsuits follow.

programme.title: South Park: The Biggest Douche in the Universe
programme.desc: When a famous psychic fails to help him exorcise Kenny from his body, Cartman and Chef travel to the moors of Scotland, where Chef's mom tries a little of her voodoo magic on him.

programme.title: South Park: It Hits the Fan
programme.desc: When the HBC Network allows a show to utter curse words on the air, it threatens to destroy the world. It's up to the Knights of Standards and Practices, as well as Cartman, Stan, Kyle and Kenny, to censor the airwaves and save the world.

programme.title: South Park: Imaginationland
programme.desc: When the entire contents of the world's imagination lay open before them, Stan and Kyle step right in. Back in South Park, Cartman swears he's seen a leprechaun.

programme.title: South Park: Imaginationland: Episode II
programme.desc: After the wall in Imaginationland is blown up by terrorists, the evil side crosses over. Cartman tracks down Kyle and Stan at the Pentagon and demands Kyle makes good on their agreement. Meanwhile, U.S. troops cross over into Imaginationland.

programme.title: South Park: Imaginationland: Episode III
programme.desc: Inside Imaginationland, Stan and Butters engage in the battle of their lives as they fight the army of evil imaginary forces. Meanwhile, Cartman goes all the way to the Supreme Court to get Kyle to pay up on their bet.

programme.title: South Park: Stunning and Brave
programme.desc: The boys express their utmost respect for Caitlyn Jenner in the most stunning and brave South Park ever.

programme.title: South Park: T.M.I.
programme.desc: Cartman throws a fit when the boys' penis sizes are posted on the school bulletin board, and is sent to anger management therapy.

programme.title: South Park: Crippled Summer
programme.desc: Jimmy and Timmy head off to summer camp with all their handi-capable friends. When one of the camp counsellors, Towelie, falls off the wagon, the boys stage an intervention.

programme.title: South Park: Mystery of the Urinal Deuce
programme.desc: Cartman exposes the true culprit behind the September 11th attacks, uncovering the world's largest conspiracy of all time. Kyle and Stan hit the road in search of the truth and come face-to-face with the masterminds who pulled off the operation.

programme.title: South Park: Eek, a Penis!
programme.desc: While Ms. Garrison is off trying to find a way to become the man he was always intended to be, Cartman is put in charge of the classroom.

programme.title: South Park: Let Go, Let Gov
programme.desc: Carman infiltrates the NSA and doesn't like what he finds in his personal file.

programme.title: South Park: Something Wall-Mart This Way Comes
programme.desc: In order to save South Park, Stan and Kyle have to find a way to destroy the ever-expanding Wall Mart superstore while keeping Cartman from stabbing them in the back.

programme.title: South Park: Summer Sucks
programme.desc: The entire town is gearing up for the annual Fourth of July celebration when a state-wide ban on fireworks is imposed. Jimbo and Ned cross the border into Mexico to get some Grade-A illegal fireworks.

programme.title: South Park: Osama Bin Laden Has Farty Pants
programme.desc: The kids of South Park have raised money to send to children of Afghanistan, but the Government believes the Afghani children's thank you gift is contaminated with Anthrax and the boys are forced to take it back to Afghanistan.

programme.title: South Park: Fishsticks
programme.desc: Cartman and Jimmy come up with the funniest joke of all time. When a humourless hip-hop star doesn't get the joke, he tries to bring justice to its creators.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: The Beast in Me
programme.desc: When an identity thief with no apparent identity of his own is found murdered in his car, the investigation leads Jane into Boston's checkered past. A startling discovery about the victim sends Maura on a personal quest.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: When the Gun Goes Bang, Bang, Bang
programme.desc: After an undercover cop working a narcotics-smuggling sting is shot dead, the killers next turn their sites on Boston homicide headquarters, leaving Jane, Maura and Frankie Jr. trapped in a fight for survival.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: We Don't Need Another Hero
programme.desc: Jane is honoured as a hero at a public event but the celebration is short-lived when disaster strikes. The ensuing case reunites Jane with an old high school flame who may be the person she needs to help her heal emotionally.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Living Proof
programme.desc: Jane and Maura's relaxing day at an outdoor spa is anything but when they discover the murder of a pregnant woman serving as a surrogate mother. Soon the hunt is on the find the baby's biological parents.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Sailor Man
programme.desc: Shortly after sailors invade the streets of Boston for Fleet Week a young girl is found dead. Frost's father, a Navy Admiral, comes to town, causing tension with his son. Maura goes on a date with Angela's mechanic.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Brown Eyed Girl

programme.title: Vengeance: Killer Neighbors: Killer Cookout
programme.desc: When police arrive on the scene of a barbeque turned bloodbath, they begin to unravel a neighborhood feud that pushed one man to the point of no return.

programme.title: Vengeance: Killer Neighbors: Fatal Fence Feud
programme.desc: Even after Lonnie Paul Tracey erected a giant barricade between him and his neighbor Tony Davis, no one ever dreamed their ongoing arguments would escalate to murder.

programme.title: Vengeance: Killer Neighbors: Blood and Terror
programme.desc: When a gay rights activist is stabbed to death in his own apartment, police are tasked with tracking down his killer, and in the process, they uncover a dark world of paranoia and delusions lurking in the heart of London.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Muddy Waters
programme.desc: Investigators believed they were looking for 2 murder weapons and 2 suspects, until further analysis of evidence set them down a different path, closer to home.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Point of Origin
programme.desc: In 1984, California was riddled with 10 arson fires in three weeks. When some evidence points to other fires further up north, the investigation widens and continues until a fingerprint is finally found.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Wired for Disaster
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman is killed instantly when a bomb explodes in her home. The bomber not only had knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Wood-Be Killer
programme.desc: A murderer thought he could cover his tracks by setting fire to the crime scene, but in doing so, he provided the forensic evidence needed to convict him; a new forensic tool used to examine the kindling proved to be his undoing.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Naughty or Nyce
programme.desc: Footprints leading away from a car accident point police in a new direction.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Going for Broke
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant stockbroker is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only clue investigators have to go on - until they're led to a client who's made some bad investments, and a dog that sheds a little more than his owner realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Skirting the Evidence
programme.desc: A woman won $5,000 at the Blackjack table then, three hours later, was kidnapped. A month after that, she was found dead.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Small Town Terror
programme.desc: Three bombings within a two-mile area were more than a coincidence, but no one claimed responsibility and no one in particular was targeted.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sharper Image
programme.desc: After a girl was killed, investigators learn that cutting-edge technologies from NASA prove that the arrested suspect was not the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Insulated Evidence
programme.desc: A young college student is found strangled and badly burned. Investigators get a break when a trace evidence expert uncovers the murder weapon in the spool of a vacuum.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Up in Smoke
programme.desc: When an elderly couple die in a housefire their son becomes the prime suspect. The son insists that he is innocent, and that his efforts to extinguish the fire made it worse. Investigators turn to science to determine the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Soiled Plan
programme.desc: Police instituted an intense search after a mother of two young children went missing. Days later they found her body. Then the case went cold for twenty years, until advances in technology allowed the investigators to see the case in a new light.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Headquarters
programme.desc: Hunters stumble upon a scene that includes a skull and small bones, bits of clothing, a woman's shoe, and a strand of hair. Police determine a woman was stabbed to death, and reconstruct an image of her face to find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: One for the Road
programme.desc: After a married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a vacation in Key West, the wife went missing and police searched the island without finding any evidence. Then two important pieces of video surfaced.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Nice Threads
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value, and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case, much less catch a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Grave Danger
programme.desc: A young man dies in a mysterious car crash, but was it an accident? Forensic science uncovered what really caused the crash, and the truth devastated three different families.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Internal Affair
programme.desc: Friends and family of a woman gone missing were determined to find her until their worst fears were confirmed weeks later. Blood evidence and computer forensics helped investigators to catch the killer, and convince the jury of his guilt.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Case of the Flue
programme.desc: In 1993, when an 83 year old woman was found dead in her home, investigators used clues like flecks of paint, t-shirt fibres, and a cellular phone to work out just how, and why, she died.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Badge of Betrayal
programme.desc: With no suspects or motives, investigators can't make sense of a murder when a young woman is found dead near the highway. A tiny piece of unique fibre on the victim's clothing will lead to the most unlikely killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Matrimony
programme.desc: A young woman claims to need some time away on her own. After 20 years go by, her family begins to believe they will never know what happened to her, until one day they receive a phone call that will alter everything.

programme.title: Dirty Jobs: Bug Breeder

programme.desc: The quiet town of Allentown, Pennsylvania implodes when two rebellious brothers become vengeful neo-Nazis and begin a rampage of hate and violence that shatters a peaceful family.

programme.title: Killer Kids: Peeping Theodore and The Six
programme.desc: A local boy became the prime suspect after an eight-year-old girl went missing in 1961. He was never charged but more than a decade later the truth comes out in a death row confession.

programme.title: Killer Cases: The Doctor is Dead
programme.desc: Dr. Teresa Sievers, a rising social media star, was found bludgeoned to death in her kitchen. Detectives began to focus on her husband Mark, who had taken out a large life insurance policy on his wife.

programme.title: Killer Cases: Murder in the Kentucky Hills
programme.desc: Shayna Hubers called 911 to report that she had killed her boyfriend in self-defence. However, investigators soon learned he was planning to date another woman, and suspected there was more to the story.

programme.title: Killer Cases: Murder Under a Blue Moon
programme.desc: After Richard Smith failed to show up for work, police discovered his dead body alongside those of his mother and brother in their home. Deputies told the media they were investigating possible links to the Wiccan religion.

programme.title: The First 48: Thicker Than Water
programme.desc: A young man is preparing to testify as a witness in an assault case when he is gunned down near his home.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Wrapper's Delight
programme.desc: The guys make a mockery of each other while acting as restaurant managers, sing along with strangers trying to name that (made up) tune. The loser gets wrapped up in a birthday surprise that definitely wasn't on his wish list.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: You're Cut Off
programme.desc: The guys pose as employees at a copy centre, conduct bizarre exit interviews for moviegoers and then lead some very peculiar protests. Later, it's unhappy hour for tonight's big loser, as he cuts off paying customers at a party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Toasted
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Sal and Q get in a pickle serving up humiliating tasks as deli waiters, and later, they step behind the counter asking customers to settle a series of silly debates. Plus, tonight's big loser gets burned during an embarrassing toast.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Brownbeaten
programme.desc: The length it took Murr's eyebrows to grow back.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Taunted House
programme.desc: The guys get extra cheesy at a pizza parlour, and then head to Wall Street where they ask strangers to help translate bizarre text messages in Spanish. Then, trapped in a haunted house, a nightmare comes to life for tonight's big loser.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: HellCopter
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q pose as unreceptive receptionists in an office waiting room, then head to a wax museum where they mingle with the stiff crowd. And in tonight’s double punishment, two losers are taken on a chopper ride from hell.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Centaur of Attention

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stuffed Turkey
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr act as not-so-handy-men assembling furniture. Thanksgiving comes early for tonight's loser when he must talk turkey at a dating event.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Blue Man Dupe
programme.desc: Murr, Joe, Q and Sal are a little too generous with fellow shoppers at the market, then ask strangers to help them out of a texting jam. Plus, the big loser fights his way, repeatedly, to the best seat in the house.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Fraudway
programme.desc: Q, Joe, Sal and Murr one-up each other while trying not to laugh, and team up for a lunch that’s for the birds. Later, tonight’s big loser hits the Broadway stage, where he’s treated to a classic New York welcome by an agitated crowd.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Well...
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr play a game of tag at a coffee shop, then convince shoppers to deliver sketchy messages at the grocery store. Plus, tonight's big loser might lose his mind in Impractical Jokers' first-ever non-punishment punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Prize Fighter
programme.desc: The guys get their checks bounced by customers who notice unusual charges, then help out coworkers with some peculiar trust exercises. Later, the big loser finds himself deep at a carnival game that's rigged for him to lose.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Alliance
programme.desc: The Jokers are challenged to hold in their laughter during presentations designed to crack them up. Plus, when Joe, Sal, and Murr try to gang up on Q, he turns the tables.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Everything's Just Rosie
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q, and Murr strike out with patrons in a bowling alley and try to force-feed unsuspecting diners. Plus, a surprise visit by Rosie O’Donnell has tonight’s loser seeing double.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Warthogs, WogHearts, and Hogwarts
programme.desc: Find out what happens when Michael tricks an off-duty cop with a fake bill, which prop kept malfunctioning and which one was used in an unaired sitcom pilot.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Drone Care
programme.desc: Find out what trick was almost cancelled due to rain; which insect attacked the magic team during rehearsal; and what is Michael's shirt size.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Top Priority of NASA
programme.desc: Find out what left a foul smell in our production office, how Michael met one of his current magic team members, and why Michael pretends to be hard of hearing. Plus, we have unaired footage.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Set Itself, Lights Itself
programme.desc: Find out how a governor helped out the show, what a good sign is that a trick was successful and how long the crew spent folding origami for a trick.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Holidays
programme.desc: Ho, ho, oh no! See what happens when Thanksgiving turkeys fight back, Santa imbibes too much holiday cheer and a leprechaun brings the luck of the Irish to a small Alabama town on St. Patrick's Day.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Motorheads 4

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Motorheads 5
programme.desc: 20 of the world's most moronic motorheads crash everything from golf carts to Segways to snowmobiles to Ferraris.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Motorheads 6
programme.desc: 20 real-life video clips of dopes driving dangerously for your amusement, including a thief in a hijacked backhoe, a trucker using Flintstone-style technology, and an Amish buggy driver who couldn't care less about the rules of the road.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Quarantine Crazy
programme.desc: As the Coronavirus pandemic grips the nation, Terry invites the family to hunker down with him at the ranch, driving everyone crazy.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Man Problems
programme.desc: Terry and the girls get involved in Rachel’s love life, resulting in a competition to find her Mr. Right. The family tries to talk Terry out of agreeing to be a potential spokesperson for a certain product.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Fit For The Fastlane
programme.desc: Terry prepares to announce at a NASCAR race, which will be his first public appearance since the pandemic started. Wanting to look his best, he tries getting back into shape while trying to convince Rachel to sing at the speedway.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Oui Oui Terr-y
programme.desc: Tammy grows suspicious of Terry’s romantic anniversary surprise trip to Paris. Meanwhile, Zurie joins in on the Bradshaw family tradition and finds her passion for showing horses.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Top Chef
programme.desc: Terry and Tammy attempt one last time to try and convince Lacey and Noah to move to Texas by offering to buy them a restaurant. Meanwhile, Rachel introduces her new guy to the family by pulling a major prank on Terry.

programme.title: The Bradshaw Bunch: Three Daughters, Three Solutions
programme.desc: Join Terry Bradshaw and his family as they navigate life.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Patriarch of Perfection
programme.desc: Meet the Chrisley family, where Todd Chrisley is king. He micromanages his family just like his business and always tries to stay five steps ahead of his five children.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Not So Sweet Sixteen
programme.desc: Savannah is turning sweet 16, and a big birthday bash is in the works. Todd has to come to grips with the fact that his little girl is growing into a young woman. At the party, Todd keeps a watchful eye on girl-crazy Chase.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Jugs and Ammo
programme.desc: Todd’s eldest daughter Lindsie broke his heart when she eloped with her now-husband, Will, without telling her family. While Lindsie recovers from plastic surgery, Todd and Will make an effort to find some common ground at the shooting range.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Two Men and a Baby
programme.desc: The Chrisleys welcome a new addition to the family: Baby Chloe. Chloe is Kyle's daughter, and she is coming to live with Todd and Julie while they teach Kyle 'Parenting 101.' Savannah and Chase get into a fender bender in Savannah's new car.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Todd-Tervention
programme.desc: The Chrisley's are off to their South Carolina lake house in celebration of Todd's mother, Faye's, birthday. To his kids' dismay, Todd confiscates all electronic devices to ensure a weekend of real family bonding.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Big Apple Blowup
programme.desc: Todd takes Savannah and Chase to New York City to teach them about the family fashion business. Savannah proves to be a perfect student, but the only thing Chase learns is how to pick up models.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Date Night
programme.desc: The Chrisley kids show their love for their parents by treating them to a special day of pampering and a romantic dinner, but planning isn't the kids' strong suit. What starts off with good intentions ends up in a comedy of errors.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Hey Chloe, It's Your Birthday!
programme.desc: Todd bets Julie he can throw just as fabulous a birthday party for Chloe as she could. The bet is on, but Todd has to do it all on his own. He soon realizes that planning cupcakes and a petting zoo is not as easy as Julie makes it look.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Rules for Dating My Teenage Daughter
programme.desc: When Julie announces she's going back to work, Todd must transform into Mr. Mom and take over the household duties. Meanwhile, Savannah feels like she's ready to start dating and pleads her case to go out with a 19-year-old American Idol.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Misadventures in Babysitting
programme.desc: Todd and Julie head out of town, and leave Chase and Savannah at home to watch Grayson overnight for the first time. They hope they will follow the rules, but Chase and Savannah have plans of their own.

programme.title: Total Bellas: First Look
programme.desc: A look at the lives of WWE stars Nikki Bella, Brie Bella, John Cena, and Daniel Bryan.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Let's Play Ball!
programme.desc: The Kardashians take on the Jacksons in a charity softball game. Meanwhile, Kris pools her resources to renovate the Watts Empowerment Center, and Khloé and Scott devise a clever plan to reveal Art Vandelay's true identity.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Lord and His Lady
programme.desc: Inspired by the March for Our Lives demonstration, Kim meets with the survivors of the Parkland shooting to learn about their movement. Meanwhile, Scott revives his podcast with Khloé in Cleveland, and Kylie feels insecure about her post-baby body.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Betrayal
programme.desc: Khloé is blindsided by some devastating news about her relationship just days before her due date. Meanwhile, Kim worries that Khloe's delivery will fall on the day of her high school reunion, and Scott and Kendall team up to do some extreme sports.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: True Story
programme.desc: The family rushes to Cleveland to be by Khloé's side while she gives birth and comes face to face with Tristan for the first time since news of his infidelity broke. Kim reconnects with old friends at her 20-year high school reunion.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Vegas, Baby!
programme.desc: Khloé and Tristan head back to LA with baby True. Unresolved tension lingers between Kim and Tristan due to his infidelity. Kourtney travels to DC to talk with Congress. Kim heads to Vegas with her girlfriends prove she's still a wild girl.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Stacking the Deck
programme.desc: Kim returns to Paris for the first time since the robbery. Meanwhile Kourtney fears she waited too long to freeze her eggs, and Kendall worries she'll humiliate herself in a charity poker tournament.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Chicago Loyalty
programme.desc: Kim's fierce loyalty to her husband becomes a source of conflict while on a trip to Chicago. Kourtney struggles with anxiety in the aftermath of her breakup, so her sisters decide to plan a girls trip to Palm Springs to cure her breakup blues.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Kourtney's Choice
programme.desc: Kim plans a trip to New York for her husband's appearance on SNL to show Kourtney how far Scott has come as a family man. At the same time, Kim contemplates an unexpected move to Chicago, and Kris fears her mother might be seriously ill.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Eat Pray Fight
programme.desc: The family heads to Bali for a relaxing vacation, but tensions flare when Kourtney accuses Khloe of complaining too much. Scott tries to find buried treasure and enlists the help of his kids. In LA, Kendall and Kylie help their mom care for MJ.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Soul(mate) Searching
programme.desc: While on a spiritual quest to find balance in Bali, Kourtney gets some news that makes her consider a future with Scott. Kim and Khloe are determined to have an insightful psychic reading but get more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Legally Brunette
programme.desc: Kim decides to follow in her father's footsteps and pursue her lifelong dream of becoming a lawyer, but worries she may have taken on more than she can handle.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Pet Peeve
programme.desc: Khloe’s mistrust of her mother’s boyfriend, Corey, resurfaces on a very awkward family trip to Palm Springs, leaving Kris hurt and confused about her daughter’s behavior. Meanwhile, the family stays with Kendall to escape the fires.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Unhappy Camper
programme.desc: Determined to fulfil one of North's lifelong dreams, Kim puts her survival skills to the test on an overnight camping trip in the wilderness. Malika heads back to Cleveland for the first time since True's birth and suspects Khloe is struggling internally.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Christmas Chaos
programme.desc: Kris reluctantly allows Kim to take over the annual Christmas Eve Party, but the holiday is in jeopardy when Kris learns Kim has invited an unwelcome guest. Khloe must come to the rescue when North’s beloved hamster unexpectedly dies and Kim can’t deal.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Heavy Meddle
programme.desc: Khole tries to mediate a fight between Kourtney and Kendall but only makes things worse. Meanwhile Kris makes herself at home in Kylie's new office space, so Kylie must show her mom who's boss.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Treachery
programme.desc: Khloe deals with some debilitating health issues, but her world is turned upside down when rumors surface of Tristan cheating with a close family friend, Jordyn Woods. Kim's husband brings his family and friends together.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: Carnival and Bollywood
programme.desc: Buddy and Duff face off in a battle of carnival treats. They must satisfy a crowd of hungry amusement park patrons. For the cake-off challenge, they must each create a Bollywood-inspired cake for Aatari Sequeira.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: It's a Dog's Life
programme.desc: Buddy and Duff put their skills to the test making pies that are delicious and beautiful enough to wow the judges. Buddy pays homage to his Old World roots, while Duff follows his artistic impulses. They create dog-themed cakes for Christina Anstead.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: Wedding Wars
programme.desc: Duff Goldman brings Buddy Valastro into his world of fine dining with a challenge to create elegant plated desserts. In the cake-off, they must create a cake for celebrity event planner Slomique Hawrylo's bridal fitting event.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: Donuts and Magic
programme.desc: Kicking things off in the bake-off challenge, pastry chefs Buddy Valastro and Duff Goldman have to handle the morning rush at Los Angeles' iconic Randy's Donuts drive-thru. They prepare dozens of deep-fried delights.

programme.title: Buddy vs. Duff: The Grand Finale
programme.desc: For the finale, pastry chefs Buddy Valastro and Duff Goldman head to the Food Network Kitchen to create their most heartfelt and monumental creations yet: chocolate desserts to conceal engagement rings for two surprise proposals.

programme.title: Kids Baking Championship: Brushstrokes and Garden Cakes
programme.desc: Embracing the brushstroke decorating trend, Valerie Bertinelli and Duff Goldman challenge the 12 kid bakers to make colorful brushstroke mini cheesecakes. Then there's a garden cake challenge with real fruit, herbs and other natural items as decorations.

programme.title: Chopped: Burgers and Bravado
programme.desc: The competing chefs are surprised by parsley cake in round two, and when the finalists focus their attention on a dessert basket that includes Cara Cara oranges and a curiously boozy condiment, the judges are impressed by the level of skill they see.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier: Legacies Live On
programme.desc: Eivin and Eve build an epic homestead-style treehouse. Atz Lee, Jane and Etienne stock their freezers with trout while Otto and August refurbish an old tractor. Atz. Sr. and Bonnie move a historic cabin onto the homestead.

programme.title: Tanked!: Just What the Doctor Ordered
programme.desc: The ATM crew recreate a spine insert for an aquarium in a rehabilitation facility's office. In Southern California, a doctor's office gets an underwater forest.

programme.title: Tanked!: Smiling Is the Best Medicine
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett join Las Vegas' First Friday street fair when they develop a photo booth into an aquarium for the 24 hour event. Across the nation at Nationwide Children's Hospital the guys create an underwater wonderland.

programme.title: Tanked!: NBA Wizardry
programme.desc: NBA All-Star John Wall asks ATM to build an aquarium shaped like a giant pair of dice. In Georgia they build a huge lighthouse aquarium for a local seafood restaurant that includes engraved dolphins.

programme.title: Tanked!: Going for the Gold(fish)
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett get into the patriotic spirit when Olympic basketball phenom Paul George gives them a call. He wants two identical fish tanks next to his indoor pond, and he wants it done by the time he gets home from the Olympics in Rio.

programme.title: Tanked!: Keyshia Cole's Dream Tank
programme.desc: R&B singer Keyshia Cole is literally dreaming of a beautiful saltwater aquarium in her home and wants to make it a reality.

programme.title: Tanked!: Howie Mandel's Surprise Tank
programme.desc: Howie Mandel has reached out to ATM for another tank. Only this time it's a surprise for his manager's company. 3 Arts Entertainment already has an existing tank but it needs a serious upgrade.

programme.title: Tanked!: The Fast and the Fishiest
programme.desc: Lusso Auto Spa designs customizations for high end and rare vehicles, now they want an exotic fish tank that matches their automobiles. They've requested a massive 14 foot, three sided wonder that will sit amidst their multi-million dollar cars.

programme.title: Tanked!: This Tank Is Ludacris!
programme.desc: Rapper and actor Ludacris is ready to get Tanked! He wants Brett and Wayde to build him a classy and sophisticated aquarium with lots of exotic fish! It's a race against the clock to get Ludacris his new aquarium.

programme.title: Tanked!: 100 Episodes STRONG!
programme.desc: ATM is excited to design a tank for WWE Superstar and tag team champion Titus O'Neil! The guys will need work as a tag team of their town in order to deliver on their promise to build a University of Florida themed Gator tank.

programme.title: Tanked!: All in the Ocean
programme.desc: Atlantic City is in for a 'fish lift.' Ocean Casino Resort wants to bring the ocean into 'Ocean.' The 6,000-gallon aquarium has ATM team literally burning through panels to get this job done.

programme.title: Tanked!: Alyssa's Charmed Tank
programme.desc: Actress, activist, and entrepreneur Alyssa Milano takes some time out her busy schedule to ask Wayde and Brett to make a tank for her family.

programme.title: Tanked!: Ty Dolla's Fresh Tank
programme.desc: Hip-hop artist Ty Dolla $ign wants a tank in his house in Long Beach. He calls in Wayde and Brett to construct a huge freshwater tank. Complete with a real rock insert, perfect replicas of Ty's awards, and embedded into a wall.

programme.desc: When Booker and Levi get into some trouble backstage at a concert, Raven and Chelsea try to seize the moment to perform with their all-time favorite group La Dee Da.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Time Crisis/ Gramma Driver
programme.desc: Remy's plan for his whole life is thrown into question when he is unprepared for a violin recital. Later, Gramma becomes a rideshare driver, only to find her best customers are thieves.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Tilly Style/ I, Farmbot
programme.desc: When Tilly is mistaken for a baby, she searches the mall for a new look to reflect her maturity. Cricket persuades Bill to buy a farming robot in order to get out of chores.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Friend Con/ Flimflammed
programme.desc: Chip Whistler and Bill hit it off and become friends at Farm Con. Cricket stumbles into having one hundred dollars and immediately buys into a pyramid scheme.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Greens' Acres/ Dolled Up
programme.desc: Young Bill will do whatever it takes to keep the family farm in business. When Tilly and Nancy celebrate Saxon's birthday at a fancy doll store, they set their sights on a seat next to Cantaloupe Sinclair, the tea party's posh VIP.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Gabriella's Fella/ Cheap Show
programme.desc: When Cricket's crush returns, Remy takes the opportunity to help Cricket get to know her.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Green Mirror/ Cricket's Tickets
programme.desc: When the Greens visit BigTech, Tilly jumps at the chance to test Gwendolyn Zapp's new technology, which promises to make them the "perfect family." Cricket wins tickets to a show and must decide whether to bring Tilly or Remy.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Times Circle/ Super Gramma
programme.desc: Tilly and Remy put on a sidewalk play while Cricket and Bill meet their favorite costumed superheroes. Cricket and Tilly try to keep Gramma at home while she recovers from eye surgery.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Quiet Please/ Chipwrecked
programme.desc: When the Greens visit the library, the librarian threatens to throw them out if they make a sound. Chip Whistler attempts to make Cricket lose his job by running Big Coffee out of business.

programme.title: Jessie: Teacher's Pest
programme.desc: When Zuri is having problems with a teacher at school, Jessie decides to volunteer in Zuri's classroom so she can assess the situation. Because Jessie has always been the teacher’s pet, she figures the teacher will love her but this isn't the case.

programme.title: Jessie: To Be Me or Not to Be Me
programme.desc: Jessie gets her first big acting part in a Shakespeare production in the park. When Jessie and Zuri switch personalities, that leaves an eight-year-old trying to pull off Jessie's performance and keep from ruining it for her.

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: Sing It On
programme.desc: Sydney really wants to attend a Rock 'n Roll summer camp but doesn't know how to tell her dad, especially since he just started a songwriter's night at the shop's café so she could work on her music.

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: Rock the Float!
programme.desc: Syd, Olive, Sophia and Emmy are excited that their design for the float honouring brave women was chosen for the annual Portland parade.

programme.title: Jessie: The Fear in Our Stars
programme.desc: Jessie has to relearn all the 'special skills' she listed on her resume for a commercial that she booked. Meanwhile, Ravi convinces Luke that a meteor is headed towards Earth.

programme.title: Jessie: Jessie Goes to Hollywood
programme.desc: When Christina Ross returns home, Jessie heads to L.A. for a role in a movie. When the kids realize that they still need her, however, they follow Jessie to Hollywood.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Town and Clown Relations
programme.desc: Lou hosts a carnival fundraiser at the camp to support her first cause as Deputy Mayor.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Smoky Flow
programme.desc: The kids form a band called the Chi-Lectives and film a video in Tess’ apartment. When things get out of hand with a smoke machine, Raven discovers they were home alone.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Twister, Sister
programme.desc: When Booker challenges Nia's efforts as an activist, she follows her dad to where he's reporting on a storm to prove to herself and her brother that she is indeed an activist and not a "positivist."

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Bro-Cave-a-Rooney
programme.desc: Joey and Parker’s room is so filthy that they try to get the girls to clean it for them. After losing a bet, the girls spend the night in the boys’ room and Liv regrets blaming her sister for losing after Maddie goes missing in Parker's tunnels.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Kathy Kan-A-Rooney
programme.desc: Liv becomes quick friends with Korean popstar and international teen sensation Kathy Kan when she comes to the states to study the actress for the Korean version of her hit show, 'Sing It Loud.'

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Burdens and Boynado

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Duets and Destiny

programme.title: Bunk'd: We Can't Bear It!

programme.desc: A film director gets akumatized into Animaestro, and is determined to show the world what animated films can do.

programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly go on a mission to find a special balloon for Keia's birthday party. When the pugs' cousin, Cody, goes missing during a game of hide-and-seek, Bingo and Rolly must find him before his family comes to pick him up.
